spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline CSS Name-only Container Queries surface (CSS, container-name, container-type); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: background-color.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener); controls include "Run capability probe"; "Toggle comparison state" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb hints at one angle but the concept page foregrounds different terminology — coverage is present but framing drifts.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5184267522015232 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on identity-inspector
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Reconcile the feature-index blurb with identity-inspector's framing
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sets up one angle but the concept page foregrounds different terminology. Either update the blurb to match what the page actually shows, or surface the blurb's framing in the page's H1/lede so a visitor lands on a coherent story.

summary: Solid concept page for CSS Name-only Container Queries: interactive, design-system clean, and references the chromestatus entry. Tighten edge-case coverage and the spec-vocabulary surface area to take it from good to canonical.
